evidence.scientific_clarity: All three required equations (ELBO, KL decomposition, RetroDiff retrieval-conditioned objective) are present with connecting prose that explains each derivation step; cross-references via \eqref work correctly.
evidence.typography: pdfTeX output uses embedded CM fonts (CMBX12, CMR10, CMMI10, CMSY10, MSBM10, CMEX10) — professional LaTeX defaults, no DejaVu Sans; math italic/upright distinction correct (\mathrm{KL}, \mathbb{E}, \mathcal{L}).
evidence.palette: No color used in this math-typesetting document; palette rubric is not meaningfully applicable, but absence of color is appropriate for a derivation block — no colorblind concerns arise.
evidence.layout_density: Single letter-page PDF with well-spaced equations and prose; no gaping whitespace or label collisions; equation numbering right-aligned as expected.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption references all three equations by \eqref label, explains each step, and includes the specific claim that R(x)=\varnothing recovers DDPM; figure and caption are consistent.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF produced by pdfTeX-1.40.29 with all fonts embedded as Type 1 subsets; pdftotext extracts full text confirming real vector content with selectable text — fonttype=42 rubric is matplotlib-specific and not applicable here.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (136KB) and figure.png (246KB) and caption.tex and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; however equations.tex (explicitly required by brief as an output) is absent from the working directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what each equation represents, names the derivation technique (Jensen's inequality, Bayes' theorem, Gaussian forward posterior), and states the specific finding that setting R(x)=\varnothing recovers the standard DDPM objective.

overall_strengths: Mathematically correct and complete derivation with all three required equations, proper LaTeX environments (align), and working \eqref cross-references throughout prose and caption.
Professional CM font stack from pdfTeX with fully embedded Type 1 subsets produces genuine vector PDF with selectable text — no rasterized math or font substitution issues.
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex is missing from the working directory despite being an explicitly required output in the brief, leaving the derivation source non-inspectable without reverse-engineering the PDF.

No color or visual hierarchy (e.g., colored equation labels, shaded boxes for the key contribution eq. 3) to help readers immediately identify RetroDiff's novel contribution versus standard DDPM background.
